title: -Contract: Mar Con Builders, Inc. for the Corporation Yard Green Room and Building H Improvements Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving plans and specifications for the Corporation Yard Green Room and
Building H Improvements Project, Specification No. 25-116706-C; and
2. Accepting the bid of Mar Con Builders, Inc. as the lowest, responsive, and
responsible bidder; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments,
extensions, or other change orders until the Project is completed in accordance
with the approved plans and specifications for an amount not to exceed
$2,170,003, which includes a contingency of $283,004.

sections.current_situation:
The Green Room and Building H are located in the City’s Corporation Yard at 1326
Allston Way. Both buildings have numerous deficiencies, and multiple systems are at
the end of their useful life, including roofing, flooring, restrooms, furnishings, electrical
systems, lighting, plumbing, and HVAC.
The project will consist of renovations to the Green Room, including new roofing,
flooring, lockers, restroom renovations, lighting, audiovisual equipment, electrical,
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

and Building H Improvements Project September 9, 2025
HVAC, and plumbing upgrades, and other related improvements. The Building H
improvements include partial re-roofing, new gates and hardware, structural
improvements, exterior lighting, and electrical upgrades.
The project was advertised for bids on January 21, 2025, and no bids were received by
the submission deadline on February 27, 2025. The project was re-issued for bids on
April 8, 2025, and on May 6, 2025, the City received four (4) bids ranging from
$1,471,253 to $2,252,001.53 for the base bid. The determination of lowest responsive
and responsible bidder was based on the price for the base bid work, as indicated in the
bid documents. The engineer’s estimate for the base bid work was $1,450,000.
On May 13, 2025, the City received two bid protests. One protest was from the second
apparent lowest bidder, Mark Lee and Yong Kay Inc., dba Bay Construction Co. (Bay)
against the bid from the apparent low bidder Mar Con Builders, Inc. (Mar Con). The
other protest was received from the apparent third lowest bidder, One Workplace
Construction LLC, dba Vantis (Vantis) against bids of the apparent lowest bidder Mar
Con and the second apparent lowest bidder Bay.
The general basis for the bid protests from V
sections.background:
The Corporation Yard is located at 1326 Allston Way and has been owned by the City
and operated as a part of its facilities for over a century. Public Works operates multiple
staffing groups from this location, including Facilities Maintenance, Electrical,
Communication, Janitorial, Equipment Maintenance and Streets and Utilities.
Additionally, the Parks Recreation and Waterfront Department operates the following
staffing groups: Forestry, Building Maintenance, and Landscape.
The Green Room serves as an assembly and training space and break room, and
houses locker rooms and restrooms for staff. Building H is a materials and equipment
storage building for the Streets and Utilities Division, has been in continual use since
the Corporation Yard was first established.
In December 2020, Council adopted a list of projects for implementation in Phase 2 of
the Measure T1 infrastructure bond program. Various facility improvements at the City
Corporation Yard were among the list of adopted projects, including to the Green Room
and Building H.
In June 2021, Public Works engaged the services of a consultant to perform condition
assessment reports at numerous City-owned facilities for deficiencies, including at the
Corporation Yard. Following the results of the assessment, the City engaged the
services of an architectural consultant to begin design of improvements to the Green
Room and Building H. Construction documents were completed and advertised for
public bid in January 2025.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no anticipated negative environmental effects of this action. The installation of
new energy efficient HVAC equipment, insulation, LED lighting, and low-flow plumbing
fixtures will comply with the latest energy efficiency and water conservation standards,
reducing energy use overall.
sections.rationale:
Contracted services are required for this project as the City does not have the in-house
expertise to complete this specialized work.

Page 4 of 6

sections.alternatives:
None. The City determined that it was necessary to move forward with the project.
Deferring the work will lead to continued deterioration of the buildings and potential
increased costs for additional repairs and maintenance.

1: Resolution

Page 4
Page 5 of 6
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: MAR CON BUILDERS, INC. FOR THE CORPORATION YARD GREEN
ROOM AND BUILDING H IMPROVEMENTS PROJECT
WHEREAS, the Corporation Yard Green Room and Building H buildings have numerous
deficiencies and need renovation; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the labor nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
project; and
WHEREAS, an invitation for bids (Plans and Specifications No. 25-11706-C) was duly
advertised, on January 21, 2025, and no bids were received by the deadline on February
27, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the invitation for bids was re-issued on April 8, 2025, bids were opened on
May 6, 2025, and the City received 4 bids; and
WHEREAS, upon receipt of two bid protests, review of the submitted bids, and careful
consideration, the basis of the protests were found to not be material and would not
significantly impact the bid; and
WHEREAS, Mar Con Builders, Inc. was determined to be the lowest responsive and
responsible bidder; and
WHEREAS, funds are programmed into the FY 2026 budget in the Measure T1 Phase 2
Fund.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that Plans
and Specifications No. 25-11706-C for the Corporation Yard Green Room and Building H
Improvements Project are approved, and that the Mar Con Builders, Inc. is determined to
be the lowest responsive and responsible bidder; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes the City
Manager to execute a contract and any amendments, extensions or change orders, until
completion of the project in accordance with the approved plans and specifications with
Mar Con Builders, Inc. for the Corporation Yard Green Room and Building H
Improvements Project at 1326 Allston Way, in an amount not to exceed $2,170,003,
which includes a 15% contingency for unforeseen conditions. A record signature copy of
the agreement and any amendments will be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
Pa
